note: Fixed 7-slide carousels, 2/day. Each post has a local 'publish_at' (interpreted in 'timezone'). The scheduler posts whatever is due and not already posted. A post only publishes when 'status' is 'ready'. Image folder name must equal the slug.

posts.caption:
You check the number after you buy, never before. There's a reason — and a two-minute fix.

Save this — looking counts as the first rep.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How long since you looked before buying? Days, weeks, years — drop it. No judgment.

posts.caption:
You don't know your balance. You know a vibe. Let's measure the gap.

Save this — looking counts as the first rep.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How far off were you? Twenty? A hundred? Drop the gap. No judgment.

posts.caption:
Your budget didn't fail because you're undisciplined. It was built to die. Here's the autopsy.

Save this for the next time yours flatlines.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: What day does yours usually die? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption:
The unread bank emails aren't laziness. They're a shame archive. Your brain is the archivist.

Save this — counting is the whole assignment.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Count yours tonight. Then drop the number here. No judgment.

posts.caption:
Open the app. Look at one number. Close it. That's the whole exercise. I'll go first.

Save this — it's your first rep.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many days since your last look? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption:
Your savings account is named 'Savings.' That's exactly why it's empty.

Save this, then go rename yours.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Go look at yours. If it still says "Savings," drop the new name here. No judgment.

posts.caption:
You muted your bank and called it self-care. Let's turn one thing back on.

Save this — deposits-only is the loophole.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How long has yours been muted? Weeks? Years? Drop it. No judgment.

posts.caption:
You set up auto-savings, then moved the money back by Friday. This loop has an off-switch.

Save this for payday morning.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: So — what day does yours crawl back? Drop the day. No judgment.

posts.caption:
There's one site that gets you at midnight. Let's make buying take ninety seconds longer.

Save this — deletion, not discipline.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Which site is it? First letter only. No judgment here.

posts.caption:
Your budget had 23 categories. You built yourself a job, then quit it. Cut it to three.

Save this — bills, life, keep.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many categories did yours have? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption:
The mid-month bill ambush isn't bad luck. Move one due date and it stops.

Save this before day 17.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: What day does yours hit — drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption:
You're paying rent on the person you were going to become. Let's cancel it together.

Save this — stop paying an old identity.

#moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many months have you paid without opening it? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption: A card with a nickname still charges interest. Save this for the next time your thumb hovers over the 'emergency' card. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Drop the month yours quietly became your main card. Mine says March. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a looking problem. Save this for the next time your thumb hovers over tap to pay. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Drop the number of times a card got declined this year when you already knew why. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. Save this for the next time the minimum feels like a finish line. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many months in a row have you paid only the minimum and told yourself it was fine? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a bill you were scared to look at. Save this so the next screenshot gets opened, not buried. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many un-opened bill screenshots are in your camera roll right now? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. Save this for the next time you drift toward the lane with no witness. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Be honest: how many times a week do you pick self-checkout so no one sees the total? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. Save this for the next time a friend waves the check away and you both say "later." #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many "figure it out laters" are floating around your life right now? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a timing trick. Save this for the next time your cart is waiting on payday. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many hours after your deposit lands before you spend? Drop the number. Mine used to be under one. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem — you had a line you kept skipping. Save this for your next statement, and read one line down. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many statements have you paid without ever reading why the number was that number? Drop a number. Mine was years of them. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a plan built for a paycheck that only shows up when everything goes right. Save this for the next slow month. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Be honest: how many months out of the last 12 did you actually hit your "normal" income number? Drop the count below. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a calendar you kept moving. Save this for the next time you tell yourself to wait for the 1st. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many "start dates" have you set and skipped this year? Drop the number. Mine was 4. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. Save this for the next time your plan starts shape-shifting at checkout. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: On a scale of 1 to 10, how far does your in-your-head plan bend when you really want the thing? Drop your number. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. Save this for the next time your tracker feels like progress. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many months have you tracked in a row without one plan coming out of it? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had an invisible label on your own money. Save this for the next time your buffer starts looking spendable. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Drop the size of the buffer you swore you would not touch, then touched anyway. Mine was $300 and lasted about nine days. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. Saving was last in line, so it never got a turn. Move a small amount on payday, before anything else, and let the leftover be the spending. Save this so future you sees it on your next payday. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Real number: how much was left to save at the end of last month? Mine once hit single digits. Drop yours. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a number your brain could not picture, so it filed the whole plan under later. Shrink the aim until step one feels boring, then move that. Save this for the next time a goal freezes you. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Drop the goal number that felt too big to start. Mine sat untouched for two years. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. Save this for the next time you catch yourself raising the number right before you hit it. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many times have you moved your own goalpost this year? Drop the number. Mine is 3. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a bill you would not look at. Today you looked. Save this for the night you keep swiping past the one you fear. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Drop the first letter of the bill you avoid most. C for card, R for rent, M for medical. I will start: M. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had three small numbers you were never allowed to see. Save this and do the two-minute rep before your next renewal. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Drop the total of your three smallest subscriptions. Mine was $9.97 and one of them I forgot I had. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. Save this for the next time you swipe the alert away. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many times have you declined this exact alert? Drop the number. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. Save this for the next time your thumb scrolls past that folder on purpose. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Drop the number of unopened receipts in your folder. I will start: mine sat at 212 before today. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had an app you hid on purpose. Save this and do the two-minute rep now. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many screens deep is your bank app right now? Drop the number. Mine was 4. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a bill you kept blurry. Save this for the next date you'd rather not name. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many bills are you keeping as 'soon' right now? Drop the number. I'll go first: 4. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a subscription you were scared to read. Save this and do the one-line rep tonight. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: How many subscriptions are you paying for that you have never read the terms of? Drop the number. Mine was 6. No judgment.

posts.caption: You never had a math problem. You had a charge nobody reminded you about. Save this for the two minutes you open your card app tonight. #moneyanxiety #financialtherapy #moneypsychology #moneymindset #personalfinance
posts.firstComment: Drop the dollar amount of the charge you forgot you agreed to. I'll start: mine was $9.99 for eight months. No judgment.
